finding: Available information quantity moderates preference direction: low-quantity (2 items) triggers direction-based selection favoring inconsistency; high-quantity (10+ items) triggers quality-based selection favoring consistency, regardless of dissonance-reduction mechanism differences.

Depleted self-regulation resources increase confirmatory information processing (preference for decision-consistent information) via heightened commitment, independent of ego threat, cognitive load, or mood; the effect is motivational rather than purely cognitive.

Implemental (planning) mind-set increases attitude strength on unrelated objects through stronger extremity, lower ambivalence, faster accessibility, and better attitude-behavior correspondence; effect is independent of deliberative mind-set or control condition.

Interpersonal risk automatically activates connectedness goals for both low and high self-esteem; high self-esteem individuals execute connectedness goals via permissive control system; low self-esteem individuals suppress connectedness via restrictive control system, creating approach-avoidance conflict.

Perceived personal capability for similar transgression predicts forgiveness; effect is mediated by reduced severity perception, increased empathic understanding, and heightened self-offender similarity; effect is stronger in men than women and when recalled offenses match target offense in severity and type.

Pride expression nonverbal recognition generalizes across cultures and ethnicities; preliterate, isolated populations recognize pride at above-chance rates comparable to Western samples, suggesting evolved universality rather than culture-specific learned gesture; target ethnicity affects recognition via status expectancy bias.
